step_by_step_thinking:
Hand-to-knee gait observed in polio indicates impairment primarily related to the quadriceps mechanism. Since polio affects motor neurons, leading to weaker or paralyzed muscles, the inability of the quadriceps to fully extend the knee would necessitate compensation via hip and knee flexion to facilitate forward motion.
\boxed{C} To summarize:
Given hand-to-knee gait in polio patients, the critical impairment lies in the quadriceps' inability to effectively extend the knee, forcing them to rely heavily on hip and knee flexion for locomotion. Therefore, the correct option reflecting this key issue is C: Quadriceps.

\boxed{C} To summarize:
